queued_events.event_title: Lusia's eldest sister Zippora immigrates to Palestine
queued_events.event_description: Lusia's eldest sister Zippora (Tzipora) Alder immigrates to Mandatory Palestine and later convinces the rest of the family to follow.

queued_events.event_title: Weitzner family emigrates to Mandatory Palestine
queued_events.event_description: Lusia's parents and siblings emigrate to Palestine. Lusia, age 16 at the time, could not accompany them due to her young age. Her father believed he would establish himself in Eretz-Israel and later send for her.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia moves to Lwów to study vocational sewing
queued_events.event_description: Lusia moves to Lwów (Lviv) to live with wealthy relatives who owned a pharmacy. She enrolls in a prestigious vocational school called 'Klaptinovka' to learn sewing.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia becomes a mother and hotel manager in Moszyna
queued_events.event_description: After Shimon's birth, Lusia decides to supplement family income by renting and managing a magnificent hotel in Moszyna. The hotel caters mostly to Polish guests, with few Jewish patrons.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia rescues two drowning children from the river in Moszyna
queued_events.event_description: While bathing in the river with young Shimon nearby, Lusia rescues two girls (ages 4 and 5) from the water. She later learns they are Christian children, and their mother sends flowers in thanks. This incident deepens Lusia's awareness of antisemitism.

queued_events.event_title: David and Leah marry in Moszyna
queued_events.event_description: David Rapaport and Leah Weitzner marry in a 'white wedding' in the small resort town of Moszyna on a cold winter day with heavy snowfall. Leah is 18 years old, David is 24. They marry without family members present.

queued_events.event_title: World War II begins—Germany invades Poland
queued_events.event_description: Germany invades Poland, marking the start of World War II. News reaches Moszyna and David suggests Lusia travel with Shimon to his parents' home until 'the anger passes.'

queued_events.event_title: Lusia and David flee to forest at night
queued_events.event_description: Two Ukrainian collaborators arrive at Lusia's door demanding she come with them. Speaking Polish fluently and appearing as a gentile, she convinces them she is not Jewish; they leave. That night, with help from neighbor Pietrozycki, Lusia and David hide in a pit in the forest while Pietrozycki cares

queued_events.event_title: Neighbor Ivanov warns Lusia of planned action against children
queued_events.event_description: Russian gentile neighbor Ivanov, whose life Lusia had helped save during Soviet occupation, brings her warning that Germans plan to take Jewish children away. He helps arrange for Shimon to be sent to Lusia's sister-in-law in Lwów dressed in girls' clothing.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia arrives in Lwów; reunites with Shimon
queued_events.event_description: Lusia arrives late at night in Lwów, where 110,000 Jews live. She stays with the sister of the Hormak family and is reunited with Shimon the next morning. Horror rumors spread that children will be executed; Lusia resolves to act quickly to save her son.

queued_events.event_title: Shimon smuggled out of Lwów ghetto on 90th day
queued_events.event_description: Lusia witnesses trucks of children being taken from the ghetto (pretext: so parents can work; reality: to certain death). She decides this is the moment to extract Shimon. She dresses him in girls' clothing and, with great confidence, walks him out of the ghetto gates with her. She then brings him t

queued_events.event_title: Lusia finds apartment and work in Lwów; hides Shimon in wooden box
queued_events.event_description: Lusia finds an apartment at Legionow Street 24 (opposite the theater) that had belonged to Jews who moved to the ghetto. She arranges a hiding place for Shimon inside a narrow wooden box (70 cm × 1.50 m) used for storing potatoes and vegetables. She creates an entrance by removing boards connecting

queued_events.event_title: Lusia obtains forged documents; becomes 'Maria' at Maritzka Square
queued_events.event_description: Using forged documents identifying her as 'Maria,' a Pole, Lusia finds work managing a hat shop on Maritzka Square, a major commercial center in Lwów run by German manager Donner. Most shops are Jewish-owned. She speaks German with a Polish accent during the examination.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia promoted to manager of art objects shop
queued_events.event_description: Donner promotes 'Maria' to manage a larger, more prominent shop selling art objects and handicrafts from rural Polish artisans. The shop becomes a gathering place. A young Polish underground girl works there, hiding underground newspapers that publish information about German atrocities and appeals

queued_events.event_title: Shimon confined to wooden box; David sutures himself to hide circumcision
queued_events.event_description: Shimon lives in the wooden box, eating, playing, sleeping, and doing his needs inside. He does not leave the box all day for many months. Shimon is pale and understands the danger, asking his mother to 'do something so I won't be Jewish.' David, who lives separately, sterilizes a needle with fire an

queued_events.event_title: Lusia encounters gymnast classmate; maintains cover
queued_events.event_description: A Polish girl whom Lusia studied with in gymnasium recognizes her at the shop. Lusia calmly explains she is a Polish friend, speaking with forced composure about how beautiful the shop is and how pleasant the work is.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia obtains Gestapo permission to travel to Zakopane
queued_events.event_description: Donner invites 'Maria' to accompany him on a trip to Zakopane in the mountains to purchase art objects from Polish peasants. She needs Gestapo permission to travel in a car reserved for Germans. At Gestapo headquarters, a tall, imposing Gestapo officer interrogates her intensely. She answers in clea

queued_events.event_title: Lusia assists Dr. Korner's daughter; gives watch to informant
queued_events.event_description: Lusia encounters the 14-year-old daughter of Dr. Korner, a dentist murdered by Germans for possessing gold. The girl is dressed in rags. Lusia helps her with food and clothing and gives her address. A large man later stops Lusia on the street, identifying her as 'Mrs. Rapaport.' He reveals he was se

queued_events.event_title: Criminal Police detectives trail Lusia; she maintains composure
queued_events.event_description: Lusia notices men's footsteps behind her on her way to work. She thinks 'This is it! My path ends here!' But she gathers her strength and mighty will to live, increases her pace, reaches the shop, and opens it as usual. Two men enter and say 'We suspect you are not a Pole.' Lusia calmly hands them h

queued_events.event_title: Criminal Police visit again; Lusia offers hospitality and false confession
queued_events.event_description: Several months after Shimon's departure, Criminal Police knock on Lusia's door again. She offers them food and drink while they search the now-empty box. She falsely confesses that the Jewess who had stayed with her (Maria Ciccelik) was kind-hearted and that she, Lusia, had made a 'terrible mistake'

queued_events.event_title: Lusia continues life of constant tension in Lwów
queued_events.event_description: Lusia lives in continuous tension. She attends church every Sunday as cover for being 'a devout Polishwoman.' She rarely sees her husband, who lives in a small hiding place near a factory. She witnesses daily scenes of horror: Germans seizing young men and beating them; a wounded Jewish man bleeding

queued_events.event_title: Black soldier breaks into building; Lusia gives him clothes
queued_events.event_description: Late in the war, a black soldier (very dirty, with rifle) breaks into Lusia's building and demands that those in hiding raise their hands and reveal German positions. Speaking Russian, Lusia warns him that if he appears as he is, Germans will shoot him and his friends will shoot everyone in the buil

queued_events.event_title: Germans abandon Lwów; Russians take control
queued_events.event_description: After the black soldier's death, the Germans abandon Lwów and Russian forces take their place, ending the Nazi occupation of the city.

queued_events.event_title: War ends; Lusia, David, and Shimon leave Poland for Katowice
queued_events.event_description: With the end of World War II, Lusia, her husband David (Memek), and their son Shimon leave the Russian part of Poland (Lwów) and travel to Katowice as part of the repatriation process.

queued_events.event_title: David and Shimon board ship Theodor Herzl for Palestine
queued_events.event_description: Memek and Shimon join a journey organized by emissaries from the Land of Israel. They board the ship 'Theodor Herzl' with a small suitcase and many hopes. The ship is captured by the British fleet. All passengers are deported to refugee camps in Cyprus.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia travels through regions of Israel with her father
queued_events.event_description: In her first days in the Land, Lusia travels with her father through different regions of the country. A trip in the Sharon region is especially memorable—she sees green orchards full of citrus fruits (oranges, lemons, grapefruits, mandarins). The blossoming renewal of nature and stunning landscape

queued_events.event_title: Lusia and Memek find work in Israel
queued_events.event_description: Memek works with his father-in-law in a small grocery store, then later in customs. Lusia works for 11 years in the post office in the department of telegrams abroad. She arrives at 7 a.m. without a single day of being late after waking early to prepare sandwiches for her children and standing at th

queued_events.event_title: Lusia and Memek travel to West Berlin for Nazi reparations
queued_events.event_description: At the end of the 1950s, Lusia and Memek travel to West Berlin to handle personal compensation as victims of Nazi persecution. Memek wrestles internally with his conscience—part of him cannot forgive the Germans, but another part argues it would be wrong to refuse legally and rightfully due payments

queued_events.event_title: Lusia meets young German lawyer; helps Shimon with reparations claim
queued_events.event_description: In Berlin, Lusia meets a young German lawyer at the beginning of his career in the lobby of a Jewish hotel. She tells him Shimon's story and mentions that an Israeli lawyer claimed Shimon has no chance of approval. The lawyer 'lights up' and says Shimon is more entitled than anyone else. Lusia immed

queued_events.event_title: Lusia travels alone to East Berlin to visit Hitler's grave
queued_events.event_description: While in West Berlin, Lusia decides she must close the circle of the terrible years of her past by seeing Hitler's grave with her own eyes. She travels alone to East Berlin, where Hitler is buried. East Berlin is communist, still recovering from war damage with ruined buildings and destroyed infrast

queued_events.event_title: Lusia begins volunteer work with B'nai B'rith
queued_events.event_description: When the children grow up, Lusia begins volunteer activities in the 'Tachrichim' office of B'nai B'rith. She works with children aged 6-13 at a school in Tirat HaCarmel, bringing them sports equipment, organizing collective bar mitzvahs, and collecting funds through a bazaar in her home.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia sent as delegate to B'nai B'rith convention in United States
queued_events.event_description: Lusia is sent as a delegate to the 45th annual convention of B'nai B'rith held in the United States.

queued_events.event_title: Lusia's granddaughter Hadas approaches teacher Esther Weiss
queued_events.event_description: In summer 1986, Hadas (Lusia's granddaughter and Shimon's daughter) approaches her teacher Esther Weiss and tells her that 'Grandma Lusia' survived the Holocaust and has many stories, but is unable to write them down. Hadas requests that the teacher help collect these stories.

queued_events.event_title: Teacher Esther Weiss conducts interviews with Lusia
queued_events.event_description: Over several meetings lasting many hours, teacher Esther Weiss visits Lusia's private apartment and collects stories from her about her life during World War II and the Holocaust, not always in chronological order. Weiss produces a booklet called 'Lusia' based on these interviews.

queued_events.event_title: Family tree research begins
queued_events.event_description: Family tree research into the Rapaport family begins, working from archival documents and the memoir 'The Story of Lusia.'

doctrine: Free layers first. Ask Doron before any paid call.
